title: Project Drop: CourseBinder + Practice Snapshot for Formative
excerpt: Two education-focused exporters landed in the project hub: one for visible Google Classroom pages and one for visible Formative practices.
author: Charlie Han
content:
This project drop adds two education tools to the public Atrak project hub:

- **CourseBinder — AI-Ready Google Classroom Exporter**
- **Practice Snapshot for Formative**

Both are public GitHub repos. Both are MIT-licensed. Both are built around a similar idea: if a student can already see a learning page in their browser, they should be able to create a local, structured archive for studying, accessibility, review, and AI-assisted organization.

## CourseBinder: Google Classroom Without the API Maze

CourseBinder exports visible Google Classroom content into a local AI-readable archive. It is intentionally not a Google Classroom API downloader.

The privacy boundary is the point:

- no Google Classroom API
- no Drive API
- no OAuth
- no cookie access
- no token scraping
- no hosted backend

The architecture looks like this:

```txt
Logged-in Google Classroom tab
  -> Chrome extension content scripts
  -> Extension controller + download queue
  -> Optional Python native host
  -> Local archive folder
  -> Read-only MCP/search layer
```

The repo includes an MV3/WXT extension, DOM snapshot extraction, attachment classification for Drive/Docs/Sheets/Slides/YouTube/external links, browser-download fallback, and a Python native host for richer archives.

## Practice Snapshot for Formative

Practice Snapshot for Formative is smaller and stricter: a Chrome extension-only MVP that captures visible Formative practice content into a ZIP.

It captures:

- visible page text
- headings and button labels
- answer state from inputs/selects/ARIA-selected controls
- image/media metadata
- one visible-tab screenshot per page
- JSONL indexes for AI search/chunking

The ZIP shape is designed to be easy for humans and AI tools to read:

```txt
FormativeAIExport/
  manifest.json
  practice.json
  practice.md
  pages/page_001/
    page.json
    page.md
    visible_text.txt
    answers.json
    images.jsonl
    links.jsonl
    screenshot.png
    raw.html
  index/
    documents.jsonl
    chunks.jsonl
```

## What We Used

CourseBinder combines:

- WXT + React + TypeScript for the Chromium extension
- Python for native archive writing and local indexing
- browser downloads for a fallback path when the native host is not installed
- MCP-style local search/fetch concepts for later Codex/GPT workflows

Practice Snapshot uses:

- WXT + React + TypeScript
- JSZip for local ZIP creation
- Vitest + Happy DOM for extension logic tests
- Chrome active-tab capture for screenshots

## Why These Matter

These are not flashy AI demos. They are infrastructure-y tools for making messy school web apps more portable and inspectable.

The best version of this lane is not "scrape everything." The best version is:

- respect the visible browser boundary
- preserve enough structure to be useful
- keep archives local
- make the output readable by both people and tools

That is why both projects are now on the main project slider with direct ZIP downloads, project pages, screenshots, and GitHub links.
